data.title: Mondelez Shareholder Votes Test Board Independence And Plastics Oversight Priorities
data.description:
• Mondelez International (NasdaqGS:MDLZ) shareholders are set to vote at the 2026 annual meeting on a proposal calling for an independent board chairman.
  • A separate proposal asks for an objective evaluation of the company’s plastics packaging practices and related policies.
  • The board has recommended that shareholders vote against both proposals, signaling its stance on current governance and sustainability frameworks.

Mondelez International, the snacks and confectionery group behind brands such as Oreo and Cadbury, sits at the center of long running discussions on corporate governance and environmental practices. Consumer goods companies have been under pressure to address packaging waste and board independence, and these proposals bring those themes directly to the shareholder ballot for NasdaqGS:MDLZ.

For investors, the upcoming votes serve as a real time marker of how much influence governance and plastics policy concerns have within the Mondelez shareholder base. The outcomes may shape future engagement between the board and investors on oversight, risk management, and ESG transparency, which many shareholders track alongside financial results when assessing a company.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Mondelez International by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Mondelez International.

See which insiders are buying and buying and selling Mondelez International following this latest news.

These shareholder proposals arrive just as Mondelez is reporting higher Q1 2026 sales of US$10.08b and net income of US$560m, so the debate is less about short term performance and more about how the company is run and how it approaches long lived packaging issues. For you as an investor, the votes give a real time check on how much priority fellow shareholders place on an independent chair and on additional plastics reporting versus keeping the current governance and disclosure set up. The board’s decision to oppose both items, while highlighting its Lead Independent Director structure and existing packaging disclosures, indicates confidence in the current model and concern about extra cost or complexity. The size of the vote for or against each proposal can indicate how engagement might evolve, including whether larger institutions want stronger oversight tools around ESG topics alongside financial metrics such as earnings per share.

Advertisement

How This Fits Into The Mondelez International Narrative

  • The plastics evaluation proposal connects directly to the existing focus on sustainability initiatives as part of the company story, reinforcing that packaging and waste remain central issues for long term brand strength.
  • The push for an independent chair questions whether the current leadership structure is the best way to oversee execution on pricing, cocoa costs, and emerging market expansion that are key elements of the narrative.
  • The cost and scope of any additional plastics reporting, if eventually adopted, may not be fully captured in the existing storyline that concentrates on pricing, cocoa programs, and distribution growth.

Knowing what a company is worth starts with understanding its story. Check out one of the top narratives in the Simply Wall St Community for Mondelez International to help decide what it's worth to you.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • ⚠️ If a meaningful bloc of shareholders backs the independent chair proposal, it could indicate dissatisfaction with current oversight and raise questions about future governance changes.
  • ⚠️ A strong vote for more plastics reporting may point to higher expectations on packaging commitments, with potential for added reporting costs or pressure to adjust product formats over time.
  • 🎁 Clear rejection of the proposals could be interpreted as support for the existing board structure and governance practices, reducing near term uncertainty around leadership changes.
  • 🎁 Constructive engagement on plastics, regardless of the vote outcome, may help protect brand equity for Oreo, Cadbury, and other key lines as consumers focus more on packaging waste.

What To Watch Going Forward

After the 2026 annual meeting, pay close attention to the final vote tallies on both proposals and any board response, especially if support rises compared with earlier similar resolutions. Watch for comments from large institutional holders on how they interpreted the outcome, and whether Mondelez updates its governance disclosures or plastics packaging reporting in future filings. It is also useful to track how these debates line up with the company’s earnings updates, packaging initiatives, and commentary on ESG priorities during results calls.

To stay informed on how the latest news affects the investment narrative for Mondelez International, head to the community page for Mondelez International to follow the top community narratives.

data.title: Boliden (OM:BOL) Margin Decline Challenges Bullish Growth Narratives In Q1 2026
data.description:
Boliden (OM:BOL) opened 2026 with Q1 revenue of SEK 27.8b and basic EPS of SEK 13.45, alongside net income of SEK 3.8b, putting fresh numbers behind its latest production and pricing mix. The company has seen revenue move from SEK 25.8b in Q4 2024 to SEK 28.1b in Q4 2025 and then SEK 27.8b in Q1 2026, while basic EPS shifted from SEK 10.95 to SEK 15.31 and then SEK 13.45 over the same reported periods. This gives investors a clearer view of how earnings are tracking into the new year as net profit margins sit in the low double digits.

See our full analysis for Boliden.

Next is a look at how these results line up with the prevailing risk and reward narratives around Boliden, and where the numbers either back those stories or start to push against them.

See what the community is saying about Boliden

Advertisement

Margin Picture Through The Cycle

  • On a trailing basis, Boliden generated SEK 11.0b in net income on SEK 100.2b of revenue, which works out to an 11% net profit margin compared with 12.1% a year earlier.
  • Consensus narrative expects margins to improve over time. However, the recent slip from 12.1% to 11% shows that, while projects like Odda and the recycling expansions aim to reduce costs and support earnings resilience, the latest twelve month margin outcome is still below that earlier level.
    • Analysts link future margin support to automation and digitalization, but current trailing EPS of SEK 38.84 is only modestly ahead of the prior trailing figure of SEK 36.65.
    • The same consensus view sees ramp ups at Tara, Kristineberg, and Rönnskär as helpful for long term efficiency, while the data still shows five year earnings declining at about 1.1% per year.

Q1 EPS Swings And Bullish Growth Hopes

  • Quarterly EPS has been quite choppy, moving from SEK 2.02 in Q2 2025 to SEK 8.07 in Q3 2025, SEK 15.31 in Q4 2025, and then SEK 13.45 in Q1 2026, while revenue ranged between SEK 21.1b and SEK 28.1b over the same stretch.
  • Bullish investors argue that project execution and low carbon positioning can support much stronger earnings growth, and the recent move in trailing EPS to SEK 38.84 compared with SEK 33.39 a quarter earlier gives them some numerical backing, even though the past five years still show an average 1.1% earnings decline.
    • Bullish narratives point to production and cost benefits from assets like Tara, Kristineberg, Odda and Rönnskär, and the TTM net income of SEK 11.0b is higher than the SEK 9.4b figure cited as a current baseline in those growth scenarios.
    • Supporters also highlight that forecasts call for earnings growth of about 13.3% per year, which sits in contrast to the negative most recent one year earnings growth flagged in the data.
Have a closer look at how the optimistic case lines up with these earnings swings and margin trends by checking out the 🐂 Boliden Bull Case

Valuation Gap Versus Profit Trends

  • Boliden trades on a trailing P/E of 12.8x with a current share price of SEK 498.10, while the DCF fair value is given as SEK 2,316.95 and the analyst price target as SEK 547.18, at the same time that trailing net margin sits at 11% and has eased from 12.1%.
  • Bears focus on the softer margin and modest five year earnings decline, and the valuation gap to the DCF fair value of SEK 2,316.95 alongside the smaller gap to the SEK 547.18 analyst target gives them room to argue that some of the discount may reflect concerns about earnings volatility and an unstable dividend record rather than pure mispricing.
    • Critics point out that while revenue over the last twelve months totals SEK 100.2b, past five year earnings have still declined by about 1.1% per year despite this scale.
    • The bearish narrative also highlights that the latest twelve month margin of 11% is lower than the prior 12.1%, which is consistent with worries about pressure from ore grades, energy costs, and regulation.

data.title: Brown And Brown (BRO) Margin Compression Challenges Bullish Earnings Growth Narrative In Q1 2026
data.description:
Brown & Brown (BRO) kicked off Q1 2026 with total revenue of US$1.9 billion and basic EPS of US$1.27, alongside trailing twelve month figures of US$6.3 billion in revenue and US$3.53 in EPS that frame the latest quarter in a bigger context. Over recent periods, revenue has moved from US$1.2 billion in Q4 2024 to US$1.6 billion in Q4 2025 and US$1.9 billion in Q1 2026. Quarterly basic EPS has shifted from US$0.73 to US$0.78 and now US$1.27, setting up a story where investors will be weighing earnings power against how margins are holding up.

See our full analysis for Brown & Brown.

With the headline numbers on the table, the next step is to see how this earnings profile lines up with the widely followed growth, risk, and valuation narratives around Brown & Brown.

See what the community is saying about Brown & Brown

Advertisement

Net income and margins cooling from prior year

  • On a trailing basis, Brown & Brown earned US$1.1b of net income on US$6.3b of revenue, which works out to an 18.2% net profit margin compared with 21% in the prior year.
  • Analysts' consensus view points to earnings stability supported by cost discipline and debt repayment, yet the 18.2% trailing margin versus 21% a year ago and quarterly net income of US$421 million versus US$327 million in Q1 2025 shows that margin compression is sitting alongside higher profit dollars.
    • Consensus commentary highlights an improved EBITDAC margin by 110 basis points and reduced interest expense, while the reported net margin has moved lower year on year, so efficiency gains are being partially offset elsewhere in the income statement.
    • The same consensus view talks about revenue opportunities in employee benefits and CAT property, but the current 18.2% net margin suggests that any benefit from those areas is not fully flowing through to the bottom line yet.

Multi year earnings growth vs slower outlook

  • Over the past five years, earnings grew at 15.9% per year, while trailing 12 month earnings growth sits at 11.6% and forecasts point to around 8.2% earnings growth and 8.3% revenue growth per year.
  • Analysts' consensus narrative leans positive on future expansion through acquisitions and strong demand in employee benefits, but the moderation from 15.9% historical earnings growth to 11.6% recently and about 8.2% expected highlights that the growth story is becoming more gradual than the past run rate.
    • The company completed 13 acquisitions with projected annual revenue of US$36 million, which is small compared with US$6.3b of trailing revenue, so these deals may fine tune growth more than transform it.
    • Consensus expects ongoing demand for benefits consulting and broader revenue expansion, yet the forecast 8.3% revenue growth remains below broader US market forecasts cited, so investors are being asked to pay attention to quality of growth rather than sheer speed.
Brown & Brown's earnings path tells only part of the story, and many investors are watching how the community weighs growth, margins, and valuation in one place, especially after this set of numbers. See what the community is saying about Brown & Brown.

P/E premium and DCF gap to current price

  • At a share price of US$63.14, the trailing P/E of 18.8x sits above the US Insurance industry average of 11.6x but below the peer group average of 35.7x, while a DCF fair value of US$138.64 and an analyst target of US$78.33 both stand above the current price.
  • Consensus narrative notes that effective cost management and cash generation help support the case for this valuation, yet the premium P/E, 18.2% net margin that is lower than last year, and balance sheet flags around debt coverage and shareholder dilution all mean the market is weighing mixed signals rather than a one way quality story.
    • On the risk side, debt is described as not well covered by operating cash flow and shareholders were diluted over the past year, so the balance sheet is an active part of the discussion, not an afterthought.
    • On the reward side, the model DCF fair value of US$138.64 and analyst target of US$78.33 both sit above US$63.14, which contrasts with the softer margin profile and slower growth forecasts and helps explain why opinions on the current P/E premium are likely divided.

data.title: Insiders Made Right Decision To Sell As AVADA Group Dips 14%
data.description:
By selling AU$944k worth of AVADA Group Limited (ASX:AVD) stock at an average sell price of AU$0.066 over the last year, insiders seemed to have made the most of their holdings. The company’s market cap plunged by AU$1.3m after price dropped by 14% last week but insiders were able to limit their loss to an extent.

While we would never suggest that investors should base their decisions solely on what the directors of a company have been doing, logic dictates you should pay some attention to whether insiders are buying or selling shares.

AVADA Group Insider Transactions Over The Last Year

In the last twelve months, the biggest single sale by an insider was when the Founder & Executive Director, Daniel Crowley, sold AU$944k worth of shares at a price of AU$0.20 per share. While insider selling is a negative, to us, it is more negative if the shares are sold at a lower price. The silver lining is that this sell-down took place above the latest price (AU$0.09). So it is hard to draw any strong conclusion from it. Daniel Crowley was the only individual insider to sell over the last year. Notably Daniel Crowley was also the biggest buyer, having purchased AU$302k worth of shares.

Over the last year, we can see that insiders have bought 3.16m shares worth AU$302k. But insiders sold 14.36m shares worth AU$944k. Insider Ownership

Many investors like to check how much of a company is owned by insiders. Usually, the higher the insider ownership, the more likely it is that insiders will be incentivised to build the company for the long term. It appears that AVADA Group insiders own 39% of the company, worth about AU$3.0m. This level of insider ownership is good but just short of being particularly stand-out. It certainly does suggest a reasonable degree of alignment.

The fact that there have been no AVADA Group insider transactions recently certainly doesn't bother us. Our analysis of AVADA Group insider transactions leaves us cautious. The modest level of insider ownership is, at least, some comfort. data.description:

  • The stock has had a strong run, with recent total returns of 8.8% over 7 days, 50.6% over 30 days, 19.7% year to date, 42.7% over 1 year, 177.7% over 3 years and 151.3% over 5 years.

Approach 1: Atlanticus Holdings Excess Returns Analysis

The Excess Returns model looks at how much profit a company generates above the return that equity investors require, then links that to the value of its equity per share. Instead of focusing on near term earnings alone, it asks whether the business earns more than its estimated cost of equity over time.

For Atlanticus Holdings, the starting point is a Book Value of $40.79 per share and a Stable EPS estimate of $8.75 per share, based on the median return on equity from the past 5 years. The model applies a Cost of Equity of $5.57 per share, which implies an Excess Return of $3.18 per share. That excess is supported by an Average Return on Equity of 19.36% and a Stable Book Value estimate of $45.19 per share, based on weighted future book value estimates from 2 analysts.

Putting these inputs together, the Excess Returns model arrives at an intrinsic value of about $80.78 per share. Compared with the recent share price of about $79.39, this suggests the stock is roughly 1.7% undervalued, which is a very small gap.

Result: ABOUT RIGHT

Atlanticus Holdings is fairly valued according to our Excess Returns, but this can change at a moment's notice. Track the value in your watchlist or portfolio and be alerted on when to act.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for Atlanticus Holdings.

Approach 2: Atlanticus Holdings Price vs Earnings

For profitable companies, the P/E ratio is a useful way to think about what you are paying for each dollar of earnings. This makes it a practical cross check against more complex models like excess returns or discounted cash flow work.

What counts as a “normal” P/E ratio depends on how the market views a company’s growth potential and risk. Higher expected growth or lower perceived risk can support a higher multiple, while lower growth or higher risk usually line up with a lower multiple.

Atlanticus Holdings currently trades on a P/E of 10.59x. That sits above the Consumer Finance industry average P/E of 9.33x and the peer group average of 5.64x. Simply Wall St’s Fair Ratio framework goes a step further and estimates a P/E of 17.12x for Atlanticus Holdings, based on factors such as its earnings profile, industry, profit margins, market value and risk characteristics.

This Fair Ratio is designed to be more tailored than a simple comparison with peers or the broad industry because it incorporates company specific metrics as well as the sector context. Set against that Fair Ratio, the current P/E of 10.59x screens below the implied level.

data.title: Is FUJIFILM (TSE:4901) Using its North American Solar Offset to Recast Its Global ESG Story?
data.description:
